data.title: HackerStorm.com Launches Vulnerability Research Platform with Real-Time Threat Intelligence
data.description:
London, United Kingdom--(Newsfile Corp. - January 21, 2026) - HackerStorm.com today announced the launch of its vulnerability research platform designed to address critical gaps in cybersecurity threat intelligence. The platform aggregates real-time threat data from multiple sources into a unified interface, democratizing access to intelligence that has traditionally remained behind paywalls or scattered across disparate sources.

To view an enhanced version of this graphic, please visit:

https://images.newsfilecorp.com/files/10373/281107_eb852b3e746c985e_001full.jpg

The platform addresses an urgent industry challenge: research indicates that 32.1% of vulnerabilities are exploited on or before the day of CVE disclosure, yet traditional vulnerability databases only display generic CVSS scores and publication dates. Government advisories often lag behind actual exploitation by days or weeks, missing 15.5% of active exploitation activity according to recent threat intelligence data.

Comprehensive Threat Intelligence from Multiple Sources

HackerStorm.com distinguishes itself from traditional vulnerability databases by aggregating multiple threat intelligence factors beyond proof-of-concept availability such as:

  • Government advisories from CISA's Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog and international cybersecurity agencies
  • Media intelligence analyzing sentiment and exploitation reports from hundreds of cybersecurity news sources
  • Proof-of-concept tracking from GitHub repositories, security researcher blogs, and academic papers
  • Expert commentary highlighting which vulnerabilities security professionals are actively concerned about
  • Time-to-exploit statistics showing how quickly vulnerabilities move from disclosure to active exploitation

Advanced EPSS Tool Delivers Context-Aware Exploitation Predictions

HackerStorm.com features an Exploitation Prediction Scoring System (EPSS) tool that transcends generic risk ratings by incorporating business and organizational context. The tool predicts where exploitation within specific organizational environments is most likely to occur, enabling more refined threat prioritization.

This capability addresses a fundamental vulnerability management challenge: 36.8% of vulnerabilities shift from critical or high severity to medium or low when contextual factors are applied. The EPSS tool enables organizations to prioritize remediation based on business context rather than generic scores.

Multi-Role Platform Serves Diverse Security Stakeholders

HackerStorm.com translates technical CVE data into accessible business impact statements designed for multiple organizational roles:

  • Technical teams access detailed ratings and technical impact assessments to establish appropriate SLA timelines
  • CISOs and security managers review business impact assessments translated into executive-level language for informed remediation prioritization
  • Auditors and compliance professionals independently verify vulnerability management effectiveness with comprehensive data rather than selective reporting

Availability

All platform features are freely accessible at www.hackerstorm.com, with no registration requirements or usage limitations. The launch represents a significant step toward democratizing enterprise-grade threat intelligence previously accessible only to organizations with substantial security budgets.

About HackerStorm.com

HackerStorm.com is a privately owned cybersecurity platform providing open-source security tools, research, and guidance on vulnerability management. The platform serves security professionals, organizations, and researchers worldwide with accessible threat intelligence and risk assessment capabilities. For more information, visit www.hackerstorm.com.

Media Contact:

Media Relations

HackerStorm.com

Email: info@hackerstorm.com

Website: www.hackerstorm.com

To view the source version of this press release, please visit https://www.newsfilecorp.com/release/281107

data.title: Albemarle Stock Rises on Another Upgrade. Higher Lithium Prices Are Driving Shares. — Barrons.com
data.description:
Al Root

Another day, another Albemarle upgrade. The sheer number of rating changes gives investors confidence that Wall Street sees the lithium market the same way.

On Wednesday, Truist analyst Peter Osterland upgraded shares of the lithium miner to Buy from Hold. His price target went to $205 from $125. Higher lithium prices drove the change.

Benchmark lithium prices peaked at about $84,000 a metric ton in late 2022, boosted by rising demand from electric vehicles. After that, lithium production expanded, and EV demand growth slowed, which pushed lithium prices below $9,000. Now, rising demand for utility-scale battery storage has pushed recent lithium prices to more than $20,000 a ton.

An individual upgrade is typically a story for investors. It signals a change in someone's thinking and can move stocks. Things are a little different with Albemarle, though. It's the number of recent upgrades that's the story.

Albemarle has been upgraded to Buy five times in the past few weeks, according to FactSet. Now, 50% of analysts covering the company rate shares Buy, up from about 33% in November. The average Buy-rating ratio for stocks in the S&P 500 is about 55%.

The average analyst price target for Albemarle stock has gone to about $167 from $90 over that span.

Commodity prices move commodity stocks. Lithium's march higher has taken Albemarle shares up 124% over the past six months. Shares rose another 3.9% early on Wednesday, to $179.22, while the S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average were both up about 0.4%.

Those kinds of rapid gains can scare investors off. At least, Wall Street seems to see things staying strong for longer.

Along with another upgrade, Citi analyst Patrick Cunningham increased his price target to $180 from $150. Higher prices are the reason. He kept his Hold rating on the stock, for now.

Write to Al Root at allen.root@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Southern Company Ranked No. 1 in Industry on FORTUNE’s 2026 World’s Most Admired Companies List
data.description:
NORTHAMPTON, MA / ACCESS Newswire / January 21, 2026 /

Southern Company has been named to FORTUNE's list of the World's Most Admired Companies for 2026, earning the No. 1 spot in the electric and gas utilities industry. The company was recognized for its financial soundness, long-term investment value and commitment to the communities it serves.

"Southern Company is a great company, and this recognition belongs to our 28,000 teammates who show up every day with purpose and a deep commitment to serving our customers," said Chris Womack, chairman, president and CEO. "Their dedication is the engine behind our work. It's how we keep earning trust, strengthening our communities and delivering the energy our customers depend on. As we look ahead, we remain focused on serving this incredible growth we're seeing in a way that benefits all customers and communities while leading the way to a stronger, more resilient energy future."

The company has a longstanding history of industry-leading innovation, resilience and a deep commitment to the people and communities it serves. Key highlights of the company include:

  • Strengthening service and infrastructure through historic investment: With more than $80 billion in investments planned through the end of the decade, Southern Company is modernizing and reinforcing its electric and gas networks to enhance reliability and resilience-with a goal of providing a strong, flexible energy foundation for future economic growth. The company expects to double the investment it has on the ground today over the next five years.

  • Serving growing demand and delivering value for customers: In a period with some of the most significant projected growth in its history, Southern Company is focused on doing what the energy industry was built to do-support economic prosperity and serve extraordinary growth. The company is meeting increasing energy needs while continuing to deliver strong value to customers. In the jurisdictions it serves, projected growth has allowed its operating companies to keep base rates stable for customers, helping to keep costs down for the families and businesses it serves. Southern Company is positioned to continue leading the way.

  • Disciplined financial management: Southern Company continues to deliver consistent value for investors, having paid a dividend equal to or greater than the previous quarter for 78 consecutive years. This track record underscores the company's disciplined financial management and long‑standing commitment to stable, dependable performance.

FORTUNE's World's Most Admired Companies list is widely regarded as the definitive benchmark on corporate reputation. Since 1997, the rankings have highlighted the business practices and leadership qualities that distinguish the most respected companies around the globe. The rankings are based on an extensive evaluation of nine key attributes, including innovation, people management, use of corporate assets, social responsibility, quality of management, financial soundness, long-term investment, quality of products and services, and global competitiveness.

Southern Company's inclusion on the 2026 World's Most Admired Companies list builds on the company's recent recognitions as the No. 1 Employer in the US on 2025 Military Times Best for Vets: Employers List and the highest-ranked U.S. energy company on Newsweek's World's Most Trustworthy Companies 2025 list. Further details regarding recent accolades collected by the company can be found on the Southern Company website.

View additional multimedia and more ESG storytelling from Southern Company on 3blmedia.com.

Contact Info:

Spokesperson: Southern Company

Website: https://www.3blmedia.com/profiles/southern-company

Email: info@3blmedia.com

SOURCE: Southern Company

View the original press release on ACCESS Newswire

data.title: Newsom says he was blocked from speaking at Davos, blames Trump administration
data.description:
Key points:
  • Trump and Newsom have history of clashes, including National Guard deployment
  • Treasury Secretary Bessent mocks Newsom's character and economic knowledge
  • Fortune says US venue made decision to not accommodate Newsom

By Steve Holland and Christian Martinez

California Governor Gavin Newsom, an outspoken Democratic critic of President Donald Trump, said he was blocked from speaking at the World Economic Forum at Davos on Wednesday, accusing the White House of interfering with his planned event.

"California was just denied at the USA House. Last we checked, California is part of USA," Newsom said in an X post, referring to the official U.S. venue in Davos.

"How weak and pathetic do you have to be to be this scared of a fireside chat?" added Newsom, who has publicly mulled seeking the Democratic presidential nomination in 2028.

Newsom's office blamed pressure from the Trump administration, without providing evidence.

"Under pressure from the White House and State Department, USA House (a church acting as the official U.S. pavilion) is now denying entry to @CAGovernor Gavin Newsom to speak with media after Fortune — the official media partner — invited him to speak," Newsom's press office said on Wednesday.

Newsom's press office said Fortune had invited him this month for a fireside chat and that Newsom accepted.

Fortune confirmed the invitation. It said in an emailed statement to Reuters that the USA House determined it would not be able to accommodate Newsom's participation and conveyed that decision to Fortune.

Trump and Newsom have clashed repeatedly since Trump took office for his second White House term a year ago, including over the Republican president's deployment of National Guard troops to the state over the summer. Trump regularly refers to the governor as "Newscum."

"I know Gavin was here," Trump said during his Davos address. "I used to get along so great with Gavin."

Newsom could be seen in the room during Trump's speech, later telling reporters, "It was remarkably boring. It was remarkably insignificant. He was never going to invade Greenland. It was never real."

Earlier Wednesday, Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent set the stage with his own mocking critique of the California governor, saying Newsom "strikes me as Patrick Bateman meets Sparkle Beach Ken," referring to, respectively, a fictional serial killer and a popular male doll that is Barbie's counterpart.

"He is too smug, too self-absorbed and too economically illiterate to know anything," Bessent said.

data.title: FedEx Stock Bounces Despite Downgrade — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Al Root

Sometimes, even good things can drive Wall Street analysts to downgrade stocks. Take FedEx on Wednesday.

Rothschild & Co Redburn analyst Oliver Holmes downgraded FedEx to Neutral from Buy, with a price target of $317 a share, close to recent levels.

FedEx shares were down in premarket trading, but brushed off the downgrade, rising 2% to $306.95 on Wednesday. It was a strong day for the market. The S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average both gained about 1.2%.

Coming into Wednesday trading, FedEx stock was up 9% over the past 12 months. Stock performance has picked up recently, with shares gaining 33% over the past six months.

One thing investors are looking forward to is the spinoff of FedEx's freight division, which is slated to wrap up in the middle of 2026.

FedEx Freight is a less-than-truckload business that serves industrial customers who generally need to move items shorter distances and don't require a full truck. Old Dominion Freight Line is a premier LTL player. Its shares typically trade for about 30 times estimated earnings expectations over the coming 12 months. FedEx stock typically trades for closer to 12 times.

FedEx Freight won't likely trade like Old Dominion on day one. Still, that valuation gap is one reason investors are excited about the spin.

The FedEx spin generated revenue of about $8.9 billion in fiscal year 2025, which ended in May, and $1.3 billion in operating income, for a margin of almost 15%. Old Dominion generated 2025 sales of about $5.5 billion and an operating profit of $1.4 billion, resulting in a 25% margin.

That value is reflected in the stock, leading Holmes to take a more cautious stance. "Investor perception towards the less-than-truckload...sector has tilted positive, bringing into focus FedEx's LTL spin-off...while also driving group valuation higher," he wrote.

Higher valuations are good things for investors, but too much of a good thing can become a risk.

About 53% of analysts covering FedEx stock rate shares Buy, according to FactSet. The average Buy-rating ratio for stocks in the S&P 500 is about 55%. The average analyst price target for FedEx stock is about $313.

Write to Al Root at allen.root@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Charles Schwab Earnings Miss Estimates. The Stock Is Rising Anyway. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Andrew Welsch

Charles Schwab shares rose on Wednesday after the financial services company reported fourth-quarter earnings that slightly missed Wall Street's expectations.

Despite that shortfall, the results showed strong growth in earnings, revenue, and net new assets, as Schwab benefited from a surge in investor activity that creates a tailwind for the company in 2026.

On Wednesday, Schwab posted adjusted fourth-quarter earnings of $1.39 per share, a penny short of the FactSet analyst consensus for $1.40. That compares with adjusted EPS of $1.01 for the year-earlier quarter. Fourth-quarter revenue came in at a record $6.34 billion, lower than analyst projections for $6.38 billion. Schwab posted revenue of $5.3 billion for the same quarter a year earlier.

Shares of Schwab were up 1.4% at about 11:30 a.m. ET Wednesday. The benchmark S&P 500 index was up 1.0%.

The Westlake, Texas-based company has been benefiting from a bull market for stocks that has enticed clients to both open more accounts and add new funds. New brokerage account openings exceeded one million for the fifth consecutive quarter, according to Schwab.

Core net new assets, a closely watched growth metric for Schwab, came in at $163.9 billion for the quarter, up from $115 billion a year earlier. For all of 2025, core net new assets totaled $519 billion, up 42% from $367 billion in 2024. The 2025 figure marks the second highest annual net new asset haul since 2021, and it brings the company's full-year organic growth rate to 5.1%, within the 5% to 7% range Schwab has been targeting. Total client assets increased 18% year over year to a record $11.9 trillion. Growth came from both of Schwab's key customer groups: individual investors and registered investment advisors.

CEO Rick Wurster told analysts that the company is adding new clients at a steady clip because it can serve a growing array of customer needs. "At our scale, we have an incredible opportunity to do more for clients at a magnitude that can't be easily replicated," he said.

The company said it expects revenue to grow by 9.5% to 10.5% in 2026 while adjusted expenses grow by a slower 5.5% to 6.5%.

Schwab has been growing its in-house wealth management offering in a bid to win more business from its millions of existing clients. Executives said that only 5% of existing client households are paying for advice but more are increasingly taking advantage of Schwab's services. The company reported that managed investing net flows last year were $68 billion compared with just $18 billion in 2022. "We expect wealth [management] to be a critical future driver of growth," Wurster said.

The company is also striving to grow its banking and lending services, particularly with registered investment advisors who rely on Schwab to safeguard client assets and provide them with a trading platform and other services. Wurster said Schwab can provide more lending capabilities for RIAs to use with their clients, positioning them to better compete with advisors at national brokerage firms such as Morgan Stanley and Merrill. About one-quarter of RIAs were using Schwab's pledged asset line as of Dec. 31.

Schwab is also expanding its alternative investment capabilities, which should get a boost once it completes its acquisition of private asset company Forge Global. It expects the deal to close during the first half of 2026.

The company also further reduced its short-term debt, with bank supplemental funding declining by $9.7 billion to end the quarter at $5.1 billion.

Even as Schwab is expanding some of its offerings, it still doesn't plan to get into prediction markets, which have proven to be a hot growth area for rivals such as Robinhood Markets. Prediction markets allow investors to wager on the outcome of events via binary yes/no contracts. Sports-related contracts are the most traded variety, and Schwab's Wurster has cautioned against the commingling of gambling and investing. On Wednesday, he said Schwab would be open to providing financial and economic-related event contracts if it would help investors achieve their goals and if there was customer demand, but that hasn't materialized yet.

"We are open to prediction markets if there is client interest, but we are not interested in sports gambling," he said. "We'll leave that to the FanDuels and Robinhoods."

Other wealth managers have also reported quarterly earnings that showed robust growth in the past year. For example, Morgan Stanley said its wealth management unit hauled in $356 billion for the full year. That compares with $252 billion for 2024. Morgan Stanley owns online brokerage E*Trade and has thousands of financial advisors.

Investors embraced risk last year and increasingly traded on margin, meaning they borrowed money from their brokerage firms to buy stocks. Margin debt rose to a record $1.22 trillion at the end of December, according to data from Finra, the brokerage industry's self-regulatory organization. Schwab's margin loan balances jumped 34% year over year to end the quarter at $112.3 billion. Daily average trading volume at Schwab was 8.3 million, up 31% compared with the same period a year ago.

If the current bull market turns into a bear market, that could crimp asset growth at Schwab and other wealth management companies. Schwab could offset some of that impact if clients increased their cash holdings with the company and if self-directed investors sought financial advice from Schwab. Meanwhile, for Schwab, it's full-speed ahead. "We are very bullish about our growth," Wurster said.

Write to Andrew Welsch at andrew.welsch@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Peru president says backlash over Chinese businessman meetings aims to derail elections
data.description:
By Marco Aquino

Peruvian President Jose Jeri told lawmakers on Wednesday that calls for his removal over undisclosed meetings with a Chinese businessman are an attempt to destabilize his government and disrupt upcoming elections.

Jeri, who took office in October after the expulsion of former President Dina Boluarte, addressed a congressional oversight committee that is investigating him for meetings with businessman Zhihua Yang on December 26 and January 6.

"There is an intention, which investigations will surely determine who is behind it, to cause harm beyond just the person, to generate instability and to alter an ongoing electoral process," the president told the committee, emphasizing the need for transparency and responsibility in clarifying matters of public interest.

Jeri's failure to publicly disclose the meetings as part of his official agenda sparked criticism over a lack of transparency and potential corruption.

Opposition legislators are trying to advance motions to impeach or censure Jeri, 39, who is carrying out the current government's term until July. The meetings are also being investigated by the public prosecutor's office.

In his defense before Congress, Jeri apologized for his off-agenda meetings with the Chinese businessman, stating that they discussed the upcoming celebration of an anniversary of Peruvian-Chinese ties. He admitted his mistake in holding the meeting in a "hidden" manner and denied receiving any "irregular" requests.

Regarding the second meeting at one of the businessman's stores selling household goods and food, Jeri said he went to buy "various products, candies, and things like paintings, normal occurrences that have been deliberately distorted."

Peru, which has had a revolving door of seven presidents since 2018 due to resignations or dismissals, is scheduled to hold elections on April 12 to choose a new president, 60 senators and 130 deputies.

data.title: RLI Q4 revenue beats expectations
data.description:
Overview

  • Specialty insurer's Q4 revenue and adjusted EPS beat analyst expectations

  • Company announced $2.00 per share special dividend, returning $183.7 mln to shareholders

  • Underwriting income for Q4 was $70.9 mln on a combined ratio of 82.6

Outlook

  • RLI did not provide specific guidance for future quarters or years

Result Drivers

  • UNDERWRITING PERFORMANCE - RLI reported $70.9 mln in underwriting income for Q4 2025, driven by a combined ratio of 82.6, reflecting strong risk management

  • RESERVE DEVELOPMENT - Favorable development in prior years’ loss reserves contributed $22 mln to Q4 underwriting income

  • INVESTMENT INCOME - Net investment income increased by 9% in Q4, supporting overall earnings growth

Key Details

Analyst Coverage

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "hold" and the breakdown of recommendations is 1 "strong buy" or "buy", 6 "hold" and 1 "sell" or "strong sell"

  • The average consensus recommendation for the property & casualty insurance peer group is "buy."

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for RLI Corp is $62.00, about 4% above its January 20 closing price of $59.61

  • The stock recently traded at 20 times the next 12-month earnings vs. a P/E of 20 three months ago

data.title: Cloud Data Files for IPO
data.description:
By Kelly Cloonan

Cloud Data Holdings has filed for an initial public offering.

The internet infrastructure service provider said it will sell about 3.8 million units at $4 to $4.50 apiece, according to a filing with the Securities and Exchange Commission on Wednesday.

The company has also granted underwriters a 45-day option to purchase up to an additional 562,500 units to cover over-allotments. Cathay Securities is listed as the offering's underwriter.

The company expects it will receive net proceeds of about $12.8 million from the offering, or about $14.8 million if the underwriters exercise their over-allotment option in full, if the offering prices at the low end of its range.

Cloud Data aims to provide digital infrastructure solutions for businesses, sourcing servers, IP addresses and bandwidth and providing connectivity services. The company also provides cloud facilitation and support services. It is headquartered in Hong Kong.

The company plans to use the proceeds to expand its data center infrastructure in Hong Kong, establish data center operations in Japan and to improve its network monitoring system.

The company intends to apply to have its units listed on the Nasdaq under the ticker CDN.

Write to Kelly Cloonan at kelly.cloonan@wsj.com
